title: Coelogyne irigensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne irigensis is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Coelogyne, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This genus is a part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which includes a large group of orchids. The Orchidaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. The subfamily Epidendroideae is further divided into several tribes and subtribes. Coelogyne irigensis is native to the Philippines, and its classification is still being studied and updated by botanists.
uses: Coelogyne irigensis, a species of orchid native to the Philippines, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are often used to treat various health conditions. In traditional Filipino medicine, Coelogyne irigensis is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antiviral and antioxidant activities. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

intro: Coelogyne irigensis is a species of orchid native to the Philippines, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and robust growth habit. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief from congestion and promote overall respiratory health. Bioactive constituents of Coelogyne irigensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Coelogyne irigensis is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions to treat respiratory conditions, and its extracts are also used in the formulation of cough syrups and expectorants.
